# terrible_f5networks_f5_modules_bigip_static_route (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) Name of the static route.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) Descriptive text that identifies the route.
- `destination` (String) Specifies an IP address for the static entry in the routing table. When creating a new static route, this value is required. This value cannot be changed once it is set.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `gateway_address` (String) Specifies the router for the system to use when forwarding packets to the destination host or network. Also known as the next-hop router address. This can be either an IPv4 or IPv6 address. When it is an IPv6 address that starts with `FE80:`, the address is treated as a link-local address. This requires the `vlan` parameter also be supplied.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `mtu` (String) Specifies a specific maximum transmission unit (MTU).
- `netmask` (String) The netmask for the static route. When creating a new static route, this value is required. This value can be in either IP or CIDR format. This value cannot be changed once it is set.
- `partition` (String) Device partition to manage resources on.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `pool` (String) Specifies the pool through which the system forwards packets to the destination.
- `reject` (Boolean) Specifies the system drops packets sent to the destination.
- `route_domain` (Number) The route domain ID of the system. When creating a new static route, if this value is not specified, the default value is `0`. This value cannot be changed once it is set.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) When `present`, ensures the static route exists. When `absent`, ensures the static does not exist.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `vlan` (String) Specifies the VLAN or Tunnel through which the system forwards packets to the destination. When `gateway_address` is a link-local IPv6 address, this value is required.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
